bullets.full: Losing on capability fit — AI fit signal unknown. No AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, or AIS_Why__c populated. deltas.subject: Multi-thread beyond Lars Kuehl by asking for introductions to the Decision Maker, Economic Buyer, and procurement leadership (e.g. Matthias Moll, Sascha Ceylan from account roster); single-threading creates ghost risk and blocks champion-building, especially on a large enterprise account with no mapped stakeholders.
Single-threaded: Lars Kuehl is the only named buyer participant, and his role/influence are unknown. Zero contact roles on the opportunity. Multi-thread or face ghost risk.

Re-engage Lars Kuehl (the only named Evonik contact from the 2026-06-24 meeting) within 3 business days (by 2026-07-01) to confirm the RFI submission deadline, ask for an introduction to the broader evaluation team (procurement lead, Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, technical evaluator), and request a discovery call to validate the pain narrative and decision criteria extracted from the RFI. The deal is currently single-threaded with an unknown contact (Lars's role, title, influence, and advocacy for Zycus are unmapped because the 2026-06-24 meeting was not recorded), creating high ghost risk. Multi-threading is the immediate priority: without access to the EB, DM, and procurement lead, Zycus cannot build a champion, map the buying committee, or de-risk the close date (2026-12-31, 188 days out). If Lars cannot or will not introduce the team, escalate to the account-level senior contacts (Matthias Moll, Head of Procurement Strategy; Sascha Ceylan, Head of Sustainability, Risk & Compliance) to open the door.

Add Lars Kuehl and any other Evonik participants discovered in the RFI review or discovery call as OpportunityContactRole entries with accurate titles and roles (Champion, Influencer, Economic Buyer, Decision Maker). Zero buyer-side contact roles creates ghost risk: if Lars Kuehl disengages or lacks influence, the deal dies with no alternate path. Multi-threading is a Day 1 discipline for enterprise deals; map the committee now.

Zero buyer-side contact roles on the opportunity. Lars Kuehl (lars.kuehl@evonik.com, attendee 24 Jun touchpoint) is the only named Evonik participant, but his title, role, and influence are unknown. No Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or Champion mapped. Single-threading creates ghost risk: if Lars disengages or lacks influence, the deal dies with no alternate path. For a $200k S2P transformation at a global enterprise, expect a multi-person buying committee (BU sponsor, central procurement, finance/CFO, IT/InfoSec). Immediate move: during the discovery call (next 3–5 days), ask Lars for the evaluation team roster and request introductions to the Decision Maker and Economic Buyer. Add all buyer stakeholders as OpportunityContactRoles.

Single-threading creates ghost risk: if Lars disengages or lacks influence, the deal dies. The move is to ask Lars on the discovery call for introductions to the evaluation team, the Decision Maker, and the Economic Buyer, and to add all buyer-side contacts as contact roles on the opportunity.

deltas.subject: Competitive landscape unknown. The RFI is almost certainly multi-vendor, meaning Evonik is benchmarking Zycus against 2-4 other S2P platforms (likely SAP Ariba, Coupa, Jaggaer, GEP, Ivalua). Zycus does not know who else is in the running, what Evonik's incumbent stack is, what gaps drove the RFI, or what our differentiation wedge should be. Without competitive intel, the RFI response and discovery positioning will be generic rather than targeted. Immediate move: review RFI for competitive clues, then ask in discovery call 'Who else are you evaluating? What are you hoping to improve vs your current approach?'
